name: Mohammad Wasim Shekh
title: Financial Analyst · Business Analytics · Data Analytics · FP&A
summary: I started in finance — a B.B.S. in Finance and two years inside a commercial bank in Nepal — then came to the U.S. and earned my degree of M.S. in Business Analytics (4.0 GPA). I learned the way to turn complex data into insights for decision making with modern tools and AI. This site is where I publish my work, so anyone evaluating me can see the thinking behind the resume bullets here.
bio: My path into finance runs through a bank branch in Birgunj, Nepal. After finishing a B.B.S. in Finance at Tribhuvan University, I spent over two years at Prime Commercial Bank — supporting credit evaluations, verifying regulatory compliance, and reconciling high-volume transactions. It taught me how finance actually works at ground level: every number has a document behind it, and accuracy is not optional.

In 2023 I moved to the U.S. for an M.S. in Business Analytics at Siena University, graduating with a 4.0 GPA and Beta Gamma Sigma honors. Since then I've applied that toolkit in the real world: at the Siena Research Institute (previously: Siena College Research Institute), I prepared and analyzed national political, economic survey datasets, automated processes, and brought survey analytical and societal insights to stakeholders and public media; at PlugPV, a solar company, I help the management in strategic decision making by analyzing business data, building SQL-driven dashboards and milestone driven financial forecasting models, while also helping with automating operational processes with CRM/API integrations, AI automation research and assisting in optimizing internal application's workflows.

What I'm building toward is the seat where those two halves compound: financial / risk analysis and FP&A roles where quantitative skills, modeling judgment and data-driven approaches matter together. I've completed the Breaking Into Wall Street financial modeling certification, and every model and research piece on this site is part of the same commitment — learning finance deeply and building the bridge between finance and advanced-modern analytical technology.

details: Beta Gamma Sigma Honors — top 20% of graduate students
Focus: Descriptive Analytics, Diagonostic Analytics, Predictive Analystcs, Prescriptive Analytics, and AI for Business Decision Making
Foundation in corporate finance, accounting, and financial statement analysis
Led directly to two years of hands-on banking experience at Prime Commercial Bank Ltd.

certifications.title: Core Financial Modeling
certifications.note: Three-statement modeling and Discounted Cash Flow valuation courses completed.

timeline.title: B.B.S. in Finance — Tribhuvan University
timeline.description: Finance first: corporate finance, accounting, and financial statement analysis in Nepal.

timeline.title: Prime Commercial Bank, Birgunj
timeline.description: Two years inside a commercial bank — credit evaluation support, compliance verification, and high-volume reconciliation. Finance at ground level.

timeline.title: Moved to the U.S. for graduate school
timeline.description: Chose an M.S. in Business Analytics at Siena University deliberately: the finance questions were already mine, the modern data toolkit wasn't yet.

timeline.title: M.S. Business Analytics — 4.0 GPA, Beta Gamma Sigma
timeline.description: Graduated top of class, then joined the Siena College Research Institute analyzing national survey data with SQL, Python, and R.

timeline.title: PlugPV — and this knowledge hub
timeline.description: Data integration, data Cleaning & mnagement, AI research, building dashboards, forecasts, and integrations at a solar company, and launching this site so the models and research have a permanent home.

timeline.title: Joined NY UPSTAT Statistics Committee
timeline.description: Joined the NY UPSTAT committee as Early Professional Amabassador for NY UPSTAT conference 2026 and presented research on comparative analysis of Vector-based and Knowledge Graph RAG at the conference.

timeline.title: Back toward finance — BIWS
timeline.description: Completed the Breaking Into Wall Street financial modeling certification alongside a growing shelf of projects.

goals: Land a full-time analyst seat — Risk Analysis, FP&A, financial analysis, or corporate finance — where models carry real decisions, ideally in energy, banking, or another data-rich industry.
Deepen the valuation and modeling craft: BIWS complete and a growing portfolio of documented models on this site.
Keep publishing — every model, research piece, and market note here is a commitment to learning finance in public.
Long term: grow into roles that bridge finance and data teams, because the analysts who speak both languages are the ones who move decisions.
